item.title: Small bowel metastasis from colorectal cancer in patients with inflammatory bowel disease: a diagnostic challenge.
item.description: BMJ case reports - Case report - Small bowel metastasis from colorectal cancer (CRC) is rare and difficult to diagnose, especially in patients with inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). We report two cases with two different presentat...

item.title: Mechanistic roles of interleukin‑18 in colonic immunity across infection, inflammatory bowel disease, and colorectal cancer (Review).
item.description: International journal of molecular medicine - Review - The colon, functioning as a central organ for digestion, absorption, and immune defense in the human body, depends on intricate interactions among the epithelial barrier, immune cells, and gut micr...

item.title: Curcumin-loaded extracellular vesicles derived from lactic acid bacteria for alleviation of inflammatory bowel disease.
item.description: International journal of pharmaceutics - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) is a chronic inflammatory disorder characterized by symptoms such as diarrhea, bleeding, and an elevated risk of colorectal cancer. Bacterial extracellular vesicles...

item.title: Exploring the Relationship Between Colitis-Associated Cancer and Lipid Metabolism Reprogramming from the Perspective of Inflammation-Cancer Transformation.
item.description: Journal of inflammation research - Review - Colitis-associated colorectal cancer (CAC) is the most severe complication of inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), characterized by multifocal lesions and poor prognosis. Aberrant lipid metabolism dri...

item.title: From Chronic Inflammation to Cancer: The Role of Trained Immunity in IBD-Associated Colorectal Carcinogenesis.
item.description: Medical sciences (Basel, Switzerland) - Review - Trained immunity is a concept that is currently in development and refers to the long-term functional reprogramming of innate immune cells in response to microbial or inflammatory stimuli. This pro...

item.title: Colorectal cancer in ulcerative colitis: unraveling mechanisms and advancing surveillance and chemoprevention.
item.description: Frontiers in oncology - Review - Patients with ulcerative colitis (UC) have an increased risk of developing colorectal cancer through inflammation-driven carcinogenesis distinct from sporadic disease. Although the relative risk re...
